A dark purple fish with white accents, flickering like a ghost in the deep.

Head to Second Sea (Isle of New Beginnings).

Equip the Great Rod of Oscar (or higher) and use Crystal Bananas as bait.

Wait for Rain weather. Fish at Night. Available during Winter.

A moderate catch that requires some preparation. Best sold to NPC for quick profit. Estimated earnings: ~15.17M C$/hr.

name: Ancient Kraken
howToCatch:
The Ancient Kraken is a Secret fish obtained by using a Poseidon's Wrath Totem while a Kraken Hunt is active, which guarantees that the Kraken will turn into an Ancient Kraken hunt, which is then simultaneously active in the Kraken Pool at a 1/567 catch rate. It can also spawn naturally when Poseidon's Wrath happens naturally when a Kraken spawns. 

The Ancient Kraken will disappear and turn back into a regular Kraken Hunt after 6 minutes. Even while an Ancient Kraken hunt is active, catching an Ancient Kraken is not guaranteed.

This is one of the more difficult  fish to obtain due to the price of the Poseidon's Wrath Totem and the chance of getting Exotic|The Kraken. 

When reeling it in, -95% Progress Speed is applied.

When hooked, a Secret will pop above the player's head, and a shimmering sound is played - as with all Secret fish.

name: Ancient Lobster
description: Ancient Lobster is a extinct fish found in Regionless. It has a base value of 13,000 C$/kg with a weight range of 20-110 kg. Appears during foggy weather. Active at night. This is a moderate catch that requires some preparation.
howToCatch:
Ancient Lobster is an Extinct fish found in Lobster Shores.
Lobsters are valuable crustaceans known for their rich, succulent meat.

Head to Regionless.

Equip the Great Rod of Oscar (or higher).

This fish is caught using Lobster Cage, not a regular fishing rod.

A moderate catch that requires some preparation. Recommended to keep for trading rather than selling to NPC. Estimated earnings: ~2.54M C$/hr.

name: Ancient Megalodon
howToCatch:
The Ancient Megalodon is an Exotic fish that can be caught near Ancient Isle during a Megalodon Hunt. It has a 10% chance to spawn when this happens. When reeling it in, -85% Progress Speed is applied.

When hooked, a Exotic will appear above the player's head, and a shimmering sound is played - as with all Exotic fish.  

The Exotic|Ancient Megalodon despawns after roughly 12 minutes if it is not caught.  

Rods with weight limits between 25,000 - 40,000 Kg need the Herculean enchantment in order to catch the Megalodon, such as Crystalized Rod, Rod Of The Depths, Firework Rod and Luminescent Oath. Rods with weight limits between 40,000 - 49,999 Kg can use the Unbreakable enchantment instead, in order to catch the Megalodon, although a rod with these weight limits does not exist.
Catching Cutscene

File:AncientMegCutscene.gif|Ancient Megalodon's cutscene

name: Axolotl
description: Axolotls are small carnivorous creatures who reside in the [[Roslit Bay#Roslit Pond
howToCatch:
The Axolotl is a Legendary fish that can be found in Roslit Bay's pond. Despite its high rarity and lower progress speed when reeling, it makes for an easy catch, moving slow and steady across the fishing bar. When reeling it in, -10% Progress Speed is applied.

When hooked, a Legendary will pop above the player's head, and a bell sound is played - as with all Legendary fish.

The Baby Nessie is required to unlock the Nessie’s Catacomb.

howToCatch: The Black Iron Bucket is a Special fish obtainable during Admin Events. It is an extremely tedious fish to obtain, as it has a -99% progress speed that cannot be changed. It is highly recommended to intentionally lose this fish during admin abuses as it takes up a lot of time.

name: Caustic Starwyrm
description: Caustic Starwyrm is a secret fish found in Brine Pool (Brine Storm). It has a base value of 43,589 C$/kg with a weight range of 21,000-23,000 kg. Best caught using Truffle Worm. Appears during rain weather. Active at night. This is a moderate catch that requires some preparation.
howToCatch:
Caustic Starwyrm is a Secret fish found in the Desolate Deep's Brine Pool during a Brine Storm.
The Caustic Starwyrm is a colossal storm-born variant of the Spectral Serpent, rarely observed outside the deepest layers of the Brine Pool during a Brine Storm, Its translucent exo-membrane is threaded with star-like bioluminescent nodes that pulse in slow, radial patterns along its body, illuminating vast sections of the abyss as it moves.

Head to Brine Pool (Brine Storm).

Equip the Stone Rod (or higher) and use Truffle Worm as bait.

Wait for Rain weather. Fish at Night.

A moderate catch that requires some preparation. Best sold to NPC for quick profit.

{{Fish|Fish_name=|image1=Cursed_Thread.png|rarity=Mythical|location=Open Ocean|bait=Mythical|Sapphire Krill|time=File:Day.png
Day|weather=File:Clear.png
Clear|season=File:Summer.png
Summer|lowest_kg=4|average_kg=7|highest_kg=21|C_kg=4|average_C=28|base_xp=20|caption-image1=Cursed Thread Render}}

The Mythical|Cursed Thread is a Mythical thread obtainable in the Open Ocean of the Second Sea. Despite its high rarity, it makes for a easy catch, moving slowly across the fishing bar. When catching, a +12% progress speed increase is inflicted on the player.

It is used in the Anti Debuff Altar as an one of the 3 main ingredients. Despite being a thread, it is sellable, therefore also tradeable to other players.

When hooked, a Mythical  will pop above the player's head, and a bell sound is played - as with all Mythical fish.

name: Cusk Eel

howToCatch:

Head to Sunken Reliquary (<!--sublocation-->). You will need a boat to reach this sea location.

Wait for Foggy weather. Fish at Night. Available during Winter.

A challenging catch — bring your best gear and time it right. Best sold to NPC for quick profit.

name: Elder Mossjaw
description: Older than memory, the Elder Mossjaw is whispered of in fearful stories. It has a moss-draped hide, and its eyes gleam with a cruel, ancient intelligence. To face it is to face the jungle’s oldest terror.
howToCatch:
The Elder Mossjaw is a  fish obtainable in an abundance located to the gate behind the center of the temple in Lost Jungle during an Elder Mossjaw hunt. Due to its high rarity, it makes for a challenging catch, moving erratically across the fishing bar (regardless of any fishing rod and/or bait, except Cinder Block Rod). When reeling it in, -85% Locked Progress Speed is inflicted. 

When hooked, a Secret will pop above the player's head, and a shimmering sound is played - as with all Secret fish.

The Elder Mossjaw Hunt has a 10% chance to replace a Mossjaw Hunt, making this effectively have a 2.5% chance to spawn every 10 minutes.

Due to it's rarity, it makes for a difficult catch, moving erratically across the bar. When reeling it in, a -92% Progress Speed is applied.

howToCatch:
Apex Fish Hunt
Narwhal is an Apex fish found in the Ocean during Narwhal Hunts.
A peculiar Arctic whale particularly known for its long, spiral tusk that resembles a unicorn horn.

Head to Ocean (Narwhal Hunt).

Equip the Stone Rod (or higher) and use Shrimp as bait.

Wait for Clear weather. Fish at Day. Available during Summer.

A moderate catch that requires some preparation. Recommended to keep for trading rather than selling to NPC. Estimated earnings: ~213.30M C$/hr.

name: Nautilus
howToCatch:
The Nautilus Is a Legendary fish obtainable in The Depths. Due to its high rarity, it makes for a challenging catch, moving erratically across the fishing bar. When reeling it in, a -10% progress speed decrease is inflicted. 

When hooked, a Legendary will pop above the player's head, and a bell sound is played - as with all Legendary fish.

A moderate catch that requires some preparation. Best sold to NPC for quick profit. Estimated earnings: ~7.5K C$/hr.

name: The Kraken
howToCatch:
The Kraken is a highly elusive Exotic fish which can be found in a Kraken Pool during a Kraken Hunt. Due to its high rarity, it makes for a challenging catch, moving erratically across the fishing bar. When reeling it in, a -85% progress speed decrease is inflicted.

When hooked, a Exotic will pop above the players head - as with all Exotic fish.

The Kraken, like the Megalodon and Secret|Scylla can be spawned through Sundial Totems. While being the quickest way, it is quite expensive. 2,000C$ per Sundial Totem and the average per Kraken is 50 or more, hence 100,000C$+.

There is a 5.2% Chance of Reeling The Kraken. (9.3% with Aurora Borealis).

When The Kraken is caught, there is a 5% chance for a Kraken Egg to drop.

name: Tidallow
description:
A tiny, bright yellow fish with striking red eyes, darting through shallow waters.
}}

== Overview ==
The Tidallow is a common fish caught in the Isle of New beginnings. Due to its low rarity, it makes it an easy catch, moving slowly across the bar with its 82 resilience. When reeling in the fish, a +14% progress speed is inflicted. 

When hooked, a ❗️will appear on top of the player's head, as with most fish. 

== Trivia ==

* Using Adventurer's Rod with the Instant Catcher bait in its preferred conditions will give the mathematically highest chance to catch the Tidallow, at a 50.2% chance.

Yellow Studfish is a Limited fish found in LEGO Pools during the LEGO Event.

Head to LEGO Event.

Equip the Scarlet Spincaster Rod (or higher) and use Stud Bait as bait.

A moderate catch that requires some preparation. Recommended to keep for trading rather than selling to NPC. Estimated earnings: ~36.9K C$/hr.

name: Yellowfin Tuna
howToCatch:
Yellowfin Tuna is an Uncommon fish found in Moosewood.
The Yellowfin Tuna is a species of tuna known for their speed and agility.

Head to Moosewood (Saltwater).

Equip the Dreambreaker (or higher).

Available during Summer.

A moderate catch that requires some preparation. Best sold to NPC for quick profit. Estimated earnings: ~583.4K C$/hr.

name: Zebrafishlet
description: Zebrafishlet is a limited fish found in Animals. It has a base value of 300 C$/kg with a weight range of 8.5-61.5 kg. Best caught using Insect. Appears during foggy weather. This is a limited fish from the Animal Hunt event. This is a moderate catch that requires some preparation.
howToCatch:
The  is a Limited Animal event fish that was added in the April Fools update. It can be caught in Animal Pools in the First Sea. Due to its low weight, it makes it an easy catch, moving sluggishly across the fishing bar and inflicting a +8% progress speed to the user when reeling it in.

When this fish is hooked, a Limited will pop up above the player's head and a ding sound is played - as with all Limited fish.

name: Zeus' Herald
description: The Zeus' Herald commands respect. Mythical rarity means you'll need good gear and some patience to catch one.
howToCatch:
Zeus' Herald is a fish in the Mythical tier, which can be caught in Zeus's Rod Room, in the body of golden water. It can be caught without a Zeus storm present. A Zeus storm can be activated via a Zeus Storm Totem.

When hooked, a Mythical will appear above the players head, and a bell sound is played - as with all Mythical fish.

name: Zombiefish
description: Once a lifeless fish drifting to the surface, the Zombiefish was struck by lightning and brought back to life. Now reanimated, it prowls the waters during FischFright, haunting the tides with its undead presence.
howToCatch:
The Zombiefish is a Limited fish obtainable during FischFright 2024, in Frightful Pools. It is an easy catch, moving slow and steady across the fishing bar.

When hooked, a Limited will pop above the player's head, and a bell sound is played - as with all Limited fish.
